from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about an athlete (or related entities) into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple handcrafted phrasing for common predicates
    phrasing = {
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {o}",
        "birthYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "height": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {o} metres tall",
        "weight": lambda s, o: f"{s} weighs {o} kilograms",
        "league": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays in the {o} league",
        "club": lambda s, o: f"{s} played for {o}",
        "currentclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently plays for {o}",
        "currentteam": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently plays for {o}",
        "position": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays as a {o}",
        "manager": lambda s, o: f"{s} is managed by {o}",
        "coach": lambda s, o: f"{s} is coached by {o}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"{s} is owned by {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"{s} is based in {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is from {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "season": lambda s, o: f"{s} participated in the {o} season",
        "foundingDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded on {o}",
        "utcOffset": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a UTC offset of {o}",
        "timeZone": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the {o} time zone",
        "areaTotal": lambda s, o: f"{s} covers an area of {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"The people of {s} are called {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
        "anthem": lambda s, o: f"The national anthem of {s} is {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"The leader of {s} is {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"The leader title of {s} is {o}",
        "mostChampions": lambda s, o: f"The team with the most championships is {o}",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"The currency of {s} is {o}",
        "demographic": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a demographic of {o}",
        "ground": lambda s, o: f"{s} has its ground at {o}",
        "stadium": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays at {o}",
        "draftPick": lambda s, o: f"{s} was drafted at pick {o}",
        "draftRound": lambda s, o: f"{s} was drafted in round {o}",
        "draftYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was drafted in {o}",
        "draftTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} was drafted by {o}",
        "debutTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} made their debut with {o}",
        "formerTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} previously played for {o}",
        "playerNumber": lambda s, o: f"{s} wears number {o}",
        "college": lambda s, o: f"{s} attended {o}",
        "youthclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} started at youth club {o}",
        "activeYearsStartYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} began their career in {o}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} passed away in {o}",
        "chairman": lambda s, o: f"{s} is chaired by {o}",
        "generalManager": lambda s, o: f"{s} is managed by general manager {o}",
        "universityTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} represents {o}",
    }

    # Build subject-object mapping for entity linking
    subject_clauses = {}
    for triple in triples:
        s = triple.subject
        if s not in subject_clauses:
            subject_clauses[s] = []
        pred = triple.predicate.lower()
        if pred in phrasing:
            clause = phrasing[pred](triple.subject, triple.object)
        else:
            clause = f"{triple.subject} {triple.predicate} {triple.object}"
        subject_clauses[s].append((clause, triple.object))

    # Build the sentence by connecting related entities
    all_clauses = []
    processed_subjects = set()

    # Find the main subject (appears most frequently or has most predicates)
    main_subject = max(subject_clauses.keys(), key=lambda s: len(subject_clauses[s]))

    # Start with main subject clauses
    for clause, obj in subject_clauses[main_subject]:
        all_clauses.append(clause)
        processed_subjects.add(main_subject)

    # Connect related entities
    for subject, clauses_list in subject_clauses.items():
        if subject in processed_subjects:
            continue
        # Check if this subject's object matches another subject
        for clause, obj in clauses_list:
            if obj in subject_clauses and obj != subject:
                # Link the clauses together
                for sub_clause, sub_obj in subject_clauses[obj]:
                    all_clauses.append(f"which is in {obj}" if sub_clause.startswith(obj) else sub_clause)
                break
            else:
                all_clauses.append(clause)
        processed_subjects.add(subject)

    # Join clauses into a single fluent sentence
    if len(all_clauses) == 1:
        sentence = all_clauses[0] + "."
    elif len(all_clauses) == 2:
        sentence = all_clauses[0] + ", and " + all_clauses[1] + "."
    else:
        # Create more natural flow with relative clauses
        main_clause = all_clauses[0]
        related = all_clauses[1:]
        sentence = main_clause
        for i, clause in enumerate(related):
            if i == len(related) - 1:
                sentence += ", and " + clause + "."
            else:
                sentence += ", " + clause + "."

    # Capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
